core_mechanism: resonance-overlap criterion for the onset of deterministic chaos: when neighbouring nonlinear resonances (here, the split components of a single resonance) overlap in the phase space of a Hamiltonian system, regular motion breaks down into chaos with exponential (Lyapunov-time) divergence of trajectories
brief_reason: The abstract attributes the chaos to 'the overlap of the components of a mean motion resonance' and quantifies it with a Lyapunov time, which is exactly the resonance-overlap route from integrable to chaotic dynamics.
